identity.title: Unified Idea â†’ Brainstorm â†’ Plan Document with Agent-Directive State Schemas
identity.summary: Merge the separate Idea row and PlanArtifact JSON into a single progressive document that advances through idea â†’ brainstorming â†’ planning â†’ reviewed â†’ accepted â†’ delivered. Each state has a machine-readable schema with an agentDirective section that prescribes agent behavior for that state. The brainstorming state schema directs the full agent-led session: question sequence, scoring formulas, synthesis update. Introduces a Brainstorm dashboard button and Brainstorming rollup. WBS decomposed into 16 single-session tasks.

goals: A single document traces an idea from inception through brainstorming, planning, review, acceptance, and delivery â€” no separate entities to keep in sync
The document status advances through six states: idea â†’ brainstorming â†’ planning â†’ reviewed â†’ accepted â†’ delivered
Each state has a machine-readable schema file with an agentDirective section that prescribes required agent behavior for that state â€” agents read the state schema and follow it, not playbook prose

The brainstorming state schema agentDirective defines the full agent-led session: ordered question phases, scoring sub-inputs, formula application steps, and synthesis update â€” agents must not invent a different flow
Agent-led brainstorming sessions are stored as an ordered array on the document and can be triggered at any document status
Each brainstorming session computes valueScore, riskScore, effortScore, confidenceScore, and priorityScore using the canonical formulas defined in the schema
The dashboard surfaces a Brainstorm button and a Brainstorming rollup showing synthesized scores (Value, Risk, Effort, Confidence, Priority) for ideas in brainstorming state
Planning schema sections (WBS, goals, non-goals) are added to the same document when the operator advances to planning

Task refs and delivery refs attach to the unified document as work progresses to delivered; a check-delivery-status command transitions acceptedâ†’delivered when all delivery task refs report complete
nonGoals: Maintaining a legacy two-artifact model (separate Idea SQLite row and PlanArtifact JSON) for any ideas after migration
Automated pipeline migration of existing ideas and plan artifacts â€” a lightweight manual agent task handles the low-volume cutover
Changing finalize-plan-to-phase task-creation logic as part of this tranche
Making agent directive weights runtime-configurable in v1 â€” weights are fixed in the schema; future iteration adds workspace-level overrides
Merging the Ideas module and Planning module into a single module (tracked as a separate improvement idea)
Automatic delivered-status detection via task-engine event hooks in v1 â€” check-delivery-status is an explicit operator/agent command

userStories.iWant: to click Brainstorm on an idea card
userStories.soThat: an agent reads the brainstorming state schema and conducts the prescribed session without me having to guide the process

userStories.iWant: to trigger a new brainstorming session on a document already in planning or later states
userStories.soThat: I can revisit assumptions and update synthesized scores without rolling back the document status

userStories.iWant: to see a Brainstorming rollup with Value, Risk, Effort, Confidence, and Priority scores
userStories.soThat: I can compare and prioritize all ideas currently in brainstorming state

userStories.iWant: my brainstorm sessions, plan WBS, task refs, and delivery refs to live on the same document
userStories.soThat: I never need to cross-reference a separate idea row and plan artifact file

userStories.iWant: to load the state schema for the current document state
userStories.soThat: I know exactly what to do next â€” which fields to populate, what questions to ask, what transitions are valid â€” without guessing

userStories.iWant: existing ideas migrated to the unified format
userStories.soThat: there is no legacy split-model debt after the cutover

valueAssessment.impact: Eliminates split-model complexity; adds agent-directive schemas that make agent behavior deterministic and repeatable; structured scoring gives a defensible prioritization basis before committing to planning
valueAssessment.rationale: Schema design and scoring model are well-understood; integration risk exists at the planning module boundary and in correctly authoring directive schemas that cover all agent edge cases

riskAssessment.description: Hard cutover leaves existing ideas and plan artifacts inaccessible until migration task runs
riskAssessment.mitigation: Migration task runs dry-run first; existing volume is low and manageable by a single agent session

riskAssessment.description: Six-state status machine with per-state required sections and agentDirective is more complex than the previous two-state idea model
riskAssessment.mitigation: Per-state schema files make each state contract explicit and machine-checkable; agents load the schema rather than inferring behavior

riskAssessment.description: Planning module commands are coupled to the two-artifact model and require non-trivial updates
riskAssessment.mitigation: WBS-3A through WBS-3C adapt commands incrementally; external argv shapes are preserved

riskAssessment.description: Incomplete or ambiguous agentDirective in the brainstorming schema could cause agents to skip questions or compute scores incorrectly
riskAssessment.mitigation: WBS-1B acceptance criteria require the directive to be walkthrough-tested with a real agent session before sign-off

riskAssessment.description: Scoring formula weights may need calibration after real-world use
riskAssessment.mitigation: Weights annotated in schema metadata; future iteration adds workspace-level overrides without changing v1 defaults

systemsTouched: schemas/ideas/states/ (six per-state schema files with agentDirective sections)
schemas/ideas/ (unified IdeaPlan envelope schema, brainstorm-session record schema)
src/modules/ideas/ (six-state machine, commands, scoring engine, state-schema loader)
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon (dashboard rollup, button labels, idea detail panel, scoring display)
technicalImpact.compatibilityNotes: Hard cutover â€” all existing I### rows and plan artifact JSON files must be migrated before the new dashboard code deploys.
technicalImpact.migrationImpact: Existing I### rows promoted to unified document format with correct state mapping; associated plan artifact JSON merged into corresponding unified documents. No legacy two-model path maintained after migration.

architecture.overview: The unified IdeaPlan document is a single progressive artifact with six status states: idea â†’ brainstorming â†’ planning â†’ reviewed â†’ accepted â†’ delivered. Each state has a dedicated machine-readable schema file under schemas/ideas/states/ that defines (a) required/optional fields at that state, (b) valid transitions, and (c) an agentDirective section prescribing agent behavior. The brainstorming state schema agentDirective is the authoritative contract for the agent-led session. Agents load the state schema and follow the directive; they do not improvise. The brainstorm section stores an ordered sessions[] array appendable at any document state; sessions are mutable in-place via update-brainstorm-session. The plan section is added at planning state. The Ideas module owns the document lifecycle. The Planning module enriches the plan section and drives plan-phase status transitions (planningâ†’reviewedâ†’accepted) via cross-module updateIdea calls. Brainstorming is optional â€” ideaâ†’planning is a valid direct transition. The unified document is stored as a linked artifact file following the existing plan-artifact convention (.workspace-kit/planning/plan-artifacts/<uuid>/artifact.vN.json); the SQLite workflow_ideas.linked_plan_artifact column holds the file reference.

decisions.decision: Hard cutover â€” no legacy two-model path after migration
decisions.rationale: Operator decision; low existing data volume makes this safe and removes long-term maintenance burden

decisions.decision: Six-state status machine: idea â†’ brainstorming â†’ planning â†’ reviewed â†’ accepted â†’ delivered
decisions.rationale: Reviewed and Accepted are first-class document statuses; Delivered replaces finalized; each state maps to a schema file

decisions.decision: Per-state machine-readable schema files with agentDirective sections â€” agents must load and follow the state schema; it is authoritative over playbook prose
decisions.rationale: Eliminates agent improvisation and ensures reproducible, comparable sessions across runs

decisions.decision: Brainstorming sessions are an array on the document â€” start-brainstorm-session appends a session at any document state
decisions.rationale: Allows assumptions to be revisited without rolling back status; only first call from idea state transitions to brainstorming

decisions.decision: Scoring formulas fixed defaults in v1 with weights annotated in the brainstorming state schema for future override support
decisions.rationale: Consistent comparable scores for rollup ranking; weights stored in schema for discoverability

decisions.decision: Storage model resolved: the unified IdeaPlan document is stored as a linked artifact file following the existing plan-artifact file convention (.workspace-kit/planning/plan-artifacts/<uuid>/artifact.vN.json); the SQLite workflow_ideas.linked_plan_artifact column holds the file reference; no new SQLite column is needed
decisions.rationale: Aligns with the existing plan-artifact pattern already used in the codebase; file-based storage supports versioned snapshots natively and keeps SQLite rows as a lightweight index; avoids JSON blob queries and large column payloads

decisions.decision: Ideas module owns the document lifecycle; Planning module enriches the plan section only
decisions.rationale: Preserves clear module ownership and prevents circular dependency

decisions.decision: Button-to-agent invocation uses the existing prefillCursorChat + buildXxxPrompt pattern: start-brainstorm-session transitions the document and appends a session slot, then the dashboard calls prefillCursorChat({ newChat: true }) with buildBrainstormSessionPrompt â€” identical to how start-idea-planning + planner-chat-prompt works today
decisions.rationale: Reuses a proven, already-shipped mechanism; no new IPC or invocation channel required

decisions.decision: IdeaStatus expanded from three values (open/planning/planned) to nine; old values remain valid SQLite strings during migration mapping openâ†’idea and plannedâ†’accepted; 'planning' is valid in both old and new models with no migration needed
decisions.rationale: Backward-compatible enum expansion avoids breaking existing callers; WBS-6 migration cleans up old values from the database

decisions.decision: PlanningChatSession records (pcs-*) survive unchanged; they track the agent chat session lifecycle for the planning phase (active/draft_ready/approval_ready/completed); the unified document status is source of truth for document state; PlanningChatSession is orchestration metadata only
decisions.rationale: Separate concerns: document state vs. agent session orchestration; no value in merging or eliminating an already-working mechanism

decisions.decision: Brainstorming is optional; ideaâ†’planning is a valid direct transition; the dashboard shows a Brainstorm button (â†’brainstorming) and a Plan button (â†’planning bypass) on idea-state cards; start-idea-planning accepts documents in idea or brainstorming state
decisions.rationale: Operators should not be forced through a full brainstorming session for simple or time-sensitive ideas; optionality also preserves existing workflow patterns

decisions.decision: priorityScore is normalized to 0â€“100 via: rawPriority = ((valueScoreÃ—0.40)+(confidenceScoreÃ—0.20)âˆ’(riskScoreÃ—0.25)âˆ’(effortScoreÃ—0.15))Ã—10; priorityScore = Math.round(((rawPriority+34)/90)Ã—100); raw range is âˆ’34 to +56 given 1â€“10 inputs; normalization maps 0â†’0, midpointâ†’50, maxâ†’100
decisions.rationale: Raw formula can produce negative values; normalized scale is intuitive for display and sorting; mid-inputs produce exactly 50 which is a useful sanity check

decisions.decision: Delivered status is triggered by an explicit check-delivery-status command that queries task engine for refs in delivery.taskRefs[]; when all tasks are completed or cancelled (minimum one completed), the command transitions acceptedâ†’delivered; automatic hooks are not implemented in v1
decisions.rationale: Automatic hooks require coupling the task engine back to the ideas module; explicit command is simpler, auditable, and correct for v1; auto-detection can be added in v2 as a reconciliation daemon

decisions.decision: Brainstorm session records are mutable in place â€” start-brainstorm-session appends a slot with sessionIndex and startedAt, update-brainstorm-session writes sub-inputs and computed scores to that slot; there is no session-level status field; sessions contain whatever the agent has written
decisions.rationale: Eliminates the atomic-session requirement that would cause data loss if the agent chat is interrupted; progressive writes are more resilient and simpler to implement

decisions.decision: Score display uses normalized 0â€“100 values with color-coded progress bars; Value, Confidence, and Priority use green-high/red-low semantics; Risk and Effort use green-low/red-high semantics; color bands: 0â€“33 red, 34â€“66 amber, 67â€“100 green; Brainstorming rollup sorted by priorityScore descending
decisions.rationale: Color semantics must match the meaning of each score (high risk is bad, high value is good); rollup ordering by priority gives the operator an immediate ranking without manual sorting

uiUxDirection.summary: Replace the Plan button on idea-state cards with two buttons: 'Brainstorm' (â†’ start-brainstorm-session then prefillCursorChat with buildBrainstormSessionPrompt) and 'Plan' (â†’ start-idea-planning direct bypass, then prefillCursorChat with buildPlannerChatPrompt). Add a secondary Brainstorm button on idea detail panels for documents in any post-brainstorming state to append a new session without changing document status. Add a Brainstorming rollup (replacing the New plan rollup) showing synthesized Value, Risk, Effort, Confidence, and Priority scores as color-coded progress bars (Value/Confidence/Priority: green-high/red-low; Risk/Effort: green-low/red-high; bands: 0â€“33 red, 34â€“66 amber, 67â€“100 green) for ideas in brainstorming state, sorted by priorityScore descending. Idea detail panel shows brainstorm session history with per-session and synthesized scores. Dashboard shows a 'Check delivery' button for accepted-state ideas.

criticalPaths: Each per-state schema validates a fixture document at that state and rejects documents missing required fields
Brainstorming state schema agentDirective walkthrough covers all 13 scoring sub-inputs and produces correct computed scores
Scoring engine produces correct outputs for all five score types given known inputs and reads weights from schema not hardcoded values
Session synthesis applies 60/40 recency weighting for Nâ‰¥2 sessions and returns session scores directly for N=1 (no weighting)
Brainstorm button transitions ideaâ†’brainstorming; secondary brainstorm appends session without status change
Full planning lifecycle (draftâ†’reviewâ†’acceptâ†’finalize) advances document status correctly at each gate
Brainstorming rollup displays correct synthesized scores from dashboard-summary projection
outOfScopeTesting: E2E browser automation for the dashboard in v1 tranche

implementationGuidance: Deliver WBS-1A first â€” all other items depend on the established TypeScript types; the storage model is already decided (file-based artifact pattern, D6) and WBS-1A documents it in an ADR
WBS-1B (brainstorming agentDirective) and WBS-1C (other 5 state schemas) can run in parallel after WBS-1A

Canonical scoring formulas (v1): valueScore=(strategicAlignmentÃ—0.35)+(customerImpactÃ—0.40)+(businessValueÃ—0.25); riskScore=(technicalRiskÃ—0.40)+(businessRiskÃ—0.35)+(dependencyRiskÃ—0.25); effortScore=(effortTshirtÃ—0.45)+(technicalComplexityÃ—0.30)+(scopeBreadthÃ—0.15)+(unknownDensityÃ—0.10) where S=2,M=4,L=7,XL=10; confidenceScore=(problemClarity+solutionClarity+feasibilityConfidence)/3; rawPriority=((valueScoreÃ—0.40)+(confidenceScoreÃ—0.20)âˆ’(riskScoreÃ—0.25)âˆ’(effortScoreÃ—0.15))Ã—10; priorityScore=Math.round(((rawPriority+34)/90)Ã—100) [range: 0â€“100]. Note: effortScore blends T-shirt size with complexity sub-inputs intentionally â€” a high-complexity M item will score higher than a low-complexity L item; this is by design and should be surfaced in agentDirective guidance.

Session synthesis formula: if sessions.length===1, synthesized scores equal the single session scores (no recency weighting); if sessions.length>=2, synthesizedScore=latestSessionÃ—0.60+mean(priorSessions)Ã—0.40 per score type; embed verbatim (including N=1 edge case) in brainstorming state schema
State-schema loader must be a reusable utility â€” given a document status, returns the schema file path and parsed directive

Button-to-agent invocation (Brainstorm button): (1) dashboard calls command client to run start-brainstorm-session for the idea; (2) reads brainstormChatPrompt from response data; (3) calls prefillCursorChat({ newChat: true }) with the returned prompt â€” identical pattern to the existing Plan button and start-idea-planning. Add buildBrainstormSessionPrompt() to playbook-chat-prompts.ts: include ideaId, title, note, sessionIndex, and instruction to load schemas/ideas/states/brainstorming.schema.json and follow the agentDirective.

Score color semantics for UI: Value, Confidence, Priority â€” green when high (good); Risk, Effort â€” green when low (good); bands: 0â€“33=red, 34â€“66=amber, 67â€“100=green. Store as named constants in extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/views/shared/ so rollup and detail panel renderers import from a single source. Rollup sorted by priorityScore descending.
whatNotToDo: Do not let agents invent question sequences or scoring logic â€” they must read and follow the brainstorming state schema agentDirective

Do not store the unified IdeaPlan document as a JSON blob on the SQLite ideas row or flatten its sections into individual columns â€” the file-based artifact pattern is canonical; the SQLite ideas row holds only the linked_plan_artifact reference
Do not allow the Planning module to own the document lifecycle or create top-level documents
Do not deploy the Brainstorm button (WBS-4B) before the backend brainstorming status and session commands are live (WBS-2A, WBS-2B)
Do not skip the recency-weighting step in synthesis â€” a flat average obscures recent reevaluation
Do not run migration without a dry-run step even though volume is low
assumptions: The agentDirective format in state schema files is JSON-serializable and loadable by the state-schema loader utility without additional tooling
All scoring sub-inputs are captured as 1â€“10 integer or decimal values; the schema agentDirective specifies the prompt and valid range for each
effortTshirt maps cleanly to numeric anchor values (S=2, M=4, L=7, XL=10) for formula consistency
The existing planning command surface can be adapted to write to the unified document plan section without a complete rewrite
Existing ideas and plan artifacts are low-volume enough for a manual agent migration task with a dry-run step
The storage model decision is resolved (file-based artifact pattern, D6); WBS-1A documents the ADR and establishes TypeScript types before WBS-1B, WBS-1C, and WBS-1D begin

wbs.title: Author brainstorming state schema with full agentDirective and walkthrough test
goalMapping: A single document traces an idea from inception through brainstorming, planning, review, acceptance, and delivery â€” no separate entities to keep in sync
Each state has a machine-readable schema file with an agentDirective section that prescribes required agent behavior for that state â€” agents read the state schema and follow it, not playbook prose

The brainstorming state schema agentDirective defines the full agent-led session: ordered question phases, scoring sub-inputs, formula application steps, and synthesis update â€” agents must not invent a different flow
Each brainstorming session computes valueScore, riskScore, effortScore, confidenceScore, and priorityScore using the canonical formulas defined in the schema

wbs.approach: Author schemas/ideas/states/brainstorming.schema.json. Required/optional fields section: define brainstorm section as required, plan section as absent. Valid transitions: brainstormingâ†’planning. agentDirective section: define ordered question phases (context, value-scoring, risk-scoring, effort-scoring, confidence-scoring, unknowns, alternatives, session-notes), each question with fieldName, prompt, type (score-1-10/text/enum), validRange, and guidance. Note in effort-scoring guidance that T-shirt size and complexity sub-inputs are weighted independently â€” a high-complexity M item intentionally scores higher than a low-complexity L. Include compute step listing all five score formulas verbatim with weights. Include synthesis-update step with explicit N=1 edge case: if sessions.length===1, synthesized=session scores; else synthesized=latestÃ—0.60+mean(prior)Ã—0.40. Note that session records are mutable in place â€” start-brainstorm-session creates the slot, update-brainstorm-session fills it progressively. Walkthrough-test the directive with a real agent session: agent must complete all phases and produce correct computed scores from the schema alone.
acceptanceCriteria: brainstorming.schema.json exists with required/optional fields, valid transitions, and complete agentDirective section
agentDirective covers all 13 scoring sub-inputs across ordered phases with prompt, type, validRange, and guidance for each
All five scoring formulas and synthesis formula (including N=1 edge case) are embedded verbatim with weights in the agentDirective compute step
effortScore guidance explicitly notes that T-shirt size and complexity sub-inputs blend intentionally
Session mutability model documented in schema: slot created by start-brainstorm-session, filled progressively by update-brainstorm-session
A brainstorming session fixture validates against the session record schema
Walkthrough test passed: agent read the directive cold and produced a complete, correctly scored session record
testingVerification: Unit test: brainstorming session fixture validates against brainstorm-session schema
Unit test: scoring formulas in directive produce correct outputs for a set of known input values
Unit test: N=1 synthesis returns session scores directly; N=2 applies 60/40 weighting
Integration walkthrough: agent completes full session from directive alone without supplementary guidance
wbs.riskNotes: The agentDirective authoring is the most creative and error-prone step in the schema tranche â€” budget time for the walkthrough test AC; this is the critical path item
wbs.doneMeans: brainstorming.schema.json committed with complete agentDirective; session fixture validates; walkthrough test passed

Follow the pattern established in WBS-1B.

wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Ideas module: six-state machine and state-schema loader utility
wbs.approach: Implement the six-state status machine in the Ideas module SQLite layer. Expand the IdeaStatus TypeScript type to IdeaPlanStatus including all nine valid values (six new states plus three legacy aliases: openâ†’idea, planning unchanged, plannedâ†’accepted). Enforce valid transitions per the transition table from WBS-1A; additionally allow ideaâ†’planning as a valid direct transition (skip brainstorming). Build the state-schema loader utility: given a document status string, returns the path to the correct state schema file and the parsed agentDirective object. No new commands in this task â€” state machine and loader only.
technicalScope: src/modules/ideas/ (six-state machine enforcement, state-schema loader utility)
acceptanceCriteria: IdeaPlanStatus type includes all six new states plus three legacy aliases; parseIdeaStatus accepts all nine values
Six-state machine implemented; all valid transitions succeed including ideaâ†’planning direct bypass; invalid transitions rejected with clear error message

wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Ideas module: start-brainstorm-session, update-brainstorm-session commands and scoring engine
wbs.approach: Implement start-brainstorm-session command: when the document is in idea state, transition to brainstorming and append a session slot with { sessionIndex, startedAt }; when the document is in any other state, append a new session slot without changing status. Implement update-brainstorm-session command: given a sessionIndex and a partial session record, merges supplied fields into the session record at that index â€” allows the agent to write sub-inputs and computed scores incrementally during the session. Implement the scoring engine: reads formula weights from the brainstorming state schema via the state-schema loader, computes valueScore, riskScore, effortScore, confidenceScore, and priorityScore (including normalization: rawPriority=((vÃ—0.40)+(cÃ—0.20)âˆ’(rÃ—0.25)âˆ’(eÃ—0.15))Ã—10; priorityScore=Math.round(((rawPriority+34)/90)Ã—100)) from session sub-inputs. Add instruction .md files for both commands.
